questions.q: Into how many branches does the Constitution divide the federal government?
questions.explanation: Legislative, executive, and judicial — the separation of powers.

questions.q: What is the system called that lets each branch limit the others?
questions.explanation: Each branch has tools to check the other two.

questions.q: How can Congress respond to a presidential veto?
options: Override it with a two-thirds vote in each chamber
Ask the Supreme Court to sign
questions.explanation: A two-thirds vote in both chambers overrides a veto.

questions.q: Which power lets courts check the other branches?
questions.explanation: Judicial review lets courts strike down unconstitutional laws or actions.

questions.q: Why did the founders separate powers?
options: To prevent any one person or branch from gaining too much power
